contact_lookup_status: No named recruiter or contact was visible in the public medmix posting checked on 2026-04-03, so the letter uses Hiring Team as the fallback.
why_company: medmix stands out to me because this role combines structured testing, documentation, PLM work, reporting, and market research in a life sciences environment where product quality and process discipline matter. I am especially interested in how the team supports the product lifecycle for medical devices while staying close to analysis, documentation, and cross-functional execution.
job_description: medmix is hiring a Werkstudent Life Cycle Management role in Stuttgart, Germany, in hybrid mode. The role supports the Product and Life Cycle Management team with performing, analyzing, and documenting various tests, including first article inspections and investigations of complaint-related products; handling changes in the PLM system; creating lifecycle reports such as validation plans and reports, ageing reports, and first article inspection documentation; conducting market and competitor research; and organizing sample and reference items independently. The posting lists a student enrollment requirement, a structured and independent working style, analytical thinking, teamwork, communication skills, MS Office skills, and solid English at B2 level. The posting does not mention German explicitly.
requirements: Enrolled at a university or university of applied sciences in medical engineering, engineering, or a related study program
Knowledge in product management, development, or technical documentation is beneficial
Self-dependent and structured way of working
Analytical mindset, teamwork, communication skills, and reliability
Confident use of MS Office applications
Solid English skills at least at B2 level
cover_letter_opening: I am applying for the Werkstudent Life Cycle Management position at medmix. The role stands out to me because it combines testing, documentation, PLM work, reporting, and market research in a structured life sciences environment where product quality and clear follow-through matter.
cover_letter_body_one: In my previous roles across product delivery and operations-heavy environments, I have worked closely with stakeholders, engineers, and business teams to structure requirements, keep documentation clear, and support execution across changing priorities. That experience made me comfortable with analytical work, process discipline, and the kind of detail-oriented coordination that lifecycle management depends on.
cover_letter_body_two: This is why the medmix role is a strong match for me. I am used to working with documentation, testing-related coordination, reporting, and research-heavy tasks that need to be organized carefully and communicated clearly. I would also value the chance to contribute in a medical-device context where lifecycle quality and product knowledge have direct impact.
cover_letter_motivation: I am especially interested in medmix because of the company’s focus on precision, innovation, and patient-centered products. The combination of product lifecycle work, analytical tasks, and life sciences context is exactly the kind of environment where I can contribute while learning quickly.
cover_letter_closing: I am available to start from 17 April 2026 and can work up to 20 hours per week in line with my current student visa. I would appreciate a quick confirmation whether German is required for this role or whether the English requirement listed in the posting is sufficient. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application with you.
